name: Hillary Clinton
subject: I’m really excited about this, Peter
body:
So, Provincetown? | Peter --

I am excited to be headed to Provincetown, Massachusetts this weekend for an event with one of my favorite performers, Cher.

Cher’s been an icon in pop music for more than fifty years -- and more than that, she’s been an advocate for LGBT equality, for AIDS research, for veterans, and for children around the world. And when the world learned of the water crisis in Flint, Michigan, Cher teamed up with a bottled water company to donate more than 180,000 bottles of water to families in that community.

Now, she’ll be joining me for an event in Provincetown on Sunday, August 21st, and it’s sure to be a good time. I’d really like you to join us, Peter -- will you add your name for the chance to be there?

No need to buy a ticket or worry about your hotel. The campaign will work that all out.

**Just add your name, and you could be there in Provincetown with me and Cher to celebrate all of the progress we’ve made:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=16&ms=MjYyODIwNDAS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYzMjk0OTQ2S0&mt=1&rt=0)
I’m really excited about this one -- I hope you can join us.

Hillary

|

It's no wonder Eric Holcomb's DC friends are going on the attack. Holcomb has run for three offices in the last six months, but hasn't released a single idea, policy proposal, anything to indicate just how he would lead the state forward -- other than saying that he'll fight to keep Indiana on the same wrong track as Mike Pence.
In the meantime, John's released plans to create good-paying jobs, make pre-K available to all Hoosier kids, fix our roads and bridges, and restore Indiana's reputation.
But if we don't have the resources to fight back against these waves of attacks -- and the special interests win -- we could be saddled with another four years of Pence-style policies. We can't let that happen.

_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about._
Do you trust Donald Trump with control of our country's nuclear codes? We sure don't, and neither do national security experts from both parties. So, we'd like to get a straight answer from Kelly Ayotte. She's refused to answer this question time and time again -- instead, she said "I think he'll surround himself, I assume, with people who will help him understand."
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd783/2a380d31/11f40e4f/fa44f5/2989713524/VEsH/ "http://www.nytimes.com/2016/08/05/science/donald-trump-nuclear-codes.html")
Ayotte needs to check her facts: The President can legally launch nuclear attacks without consulting _anyone_ -- and there is no opportunity for a Congressional check once a strike has been ordered. Want to know more? [**Read this New York Times report about why someone as volatile as Donald Trump absolutely cannot be trusted with our nuclear arsenal and national security.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd783/2a380d31/11f40e4f/fa44f5/2989713524/VEsE/ "http://www.nytimes.com/2016/08/05/science/donald-trump-nuclear-codes.html")
-- Team Maggie
P.S. -- It's clear that Kelly Ayotte puts her party over the safety and security of this country. We can't let her be a rubber stamp for Trump's dangerous agenda. body:
A right-wing infiltrator came into our campaign office this week. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf4456/1c469400/7cf0692/35733f2/1469888957/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rightwing-spy?utm_source=ema_2016-08-19_rightwingspy_nd&refcode=ema_2016-08-19_rightwingspy_nd&utm_campaign=rightwingspy&utm_medium=ema") Friend -- It all started with James O'Keefe, the notorious right-wing spy who infiltrated Planned Parenthood and tried to bring down the Obama campaign. It looks like we have our own James O'Keefe right here in Wisconsin. How do I know? **Because I'm the person who caught them.** Our campaign has had to deal with millions of dollars in misleading, negative ads from the Koch network, but this is a new low. Republicans are getting desperate, and they've resorted to the dirtiest tactic in their playbook: sending a spy into our campaign headquarters. This person came into our campaign headquarters using a fake name, a fake story, and even a fake Facebook page to try and cover their tracks. **It clearly shows what you and I already know: Republicans like Senator Johnson are scared. They're desperate. And they'll do anything to keep him in Washington for another six years.** I'm proud to work on a different kind of campaign. Team Russ is fueled by grassroots support. name: General Barry McCaffrey, USA (Ret.)
subject: I know a strong leader when I see one
body:
| Peter \--

I earned three Purple Hearts before serving as President Bill Clinton’s drug czar. I’ve been in some tough battles, so I know a strong leader when I see one.

Rob Portman is one of the finest public servants I know. He has been a leader on many issues, including the country’s growing drug epidemic. Rob has led on this issue for more than 20 years, started his own anti-drug coalition in Cincinnati, and he’s authored over a half-dozen anti-drug bills that became law.

Most recently, Rob's bipartisan Comprehensive Addiction and Recovery Act was signed into law and will address the heroin epidemic, making a historic commitment to prevention, treatment and recovery. Rob Portman is giving Ohio families hope and I am happy to support him for United States Senate. Watch the campaign's new ad by [clicking here](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3D_R3j2AP9QwA%26feature%3Dyoutu.be&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mccaffrey_blast&n=1).

[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.youtube.com%2Fwatch%3Fv%3D_R3j2AP9QwA%26feature%3Dyoutu.be&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mccaffrey_blast&n=2)
I’m excited to endorse Rob for United States Senate, and I hope you will join me in ensuring he returns to the Senate next year.

Sincerely,

General Barry McCaffrey, USA (Ret.) [](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.facebook.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mccaffrey_blast&n=3)[](https://www.robportman.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Frobportman&e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=mccaffrey_blast&n=4)

body:
This is not for a hypothetical. | Peter --

When we learn that we’re in danger of falling behind in fundraising -- when we’re not on track to hit a goal or when Trump writes himself a million-dollar check -- we come to you because you’re our A-team.

I'm not even talking about the fact that supporters like you have contributed a majority of the money we've raised -- and how important that is.

I mean we literally look to you to cover gaps when we need to go up on the air with a last-minute ad to make up for whatever horrible attack Trump has launched.

I'm talking about how you have our back when we need it most. And that's essential.

With the dual announcements that Trump’s first general-election ad buy goes live today and he just hired the executive chairman of _Breitbart News_ as one of his advisors, those attacks are going to get more and more frequent.

**So right now, I’m asking you to chip in -- not for a hypothetical, but so that we can be ready for what’s coming our way tomorrow, the next day, and all 78 days after that (and yes, we’ll send you a free sticker!):** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjYyODgwMDkS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYzNDM2NjY0S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Brian Zuzenak, HillaryClinton.com
subject: Election Day is 81 days away
body:
| Peter --

Technically, Election Day is 81 days away.

But real talk: In North Carolina and Florida, nearly 60 percent of votes will be cast before November 8th, either by mail or by folks voting early in person.

Getting supporters to vote before Election Day is important for our path to victory -- it’s both easy for voters, and it helps our campaign better plan how to allocate our resources. It’s also a real lift in terms of our organization capacity. You’ve got to know which supporters to talk to, when to engage, how to contact them, and what to ask them to do.

It requires us to be hitting on all cylinders. It takes data, analysis, field organizers, and a volunteer infrastructure that is efficient and effective without any operational hiccups.

****[That’s what you’ll be a part of when you become a volunteer today, August 19th -- it’s why you can’t wait to sign up.](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=57&ms=MjYyODkzMDQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYzNDQ5NTkyS0&mt=2&rj=ODYzNDQ5NTkyS0&rt=0)****

You could stay on the sidelines until the debates start in September. You could wait until it’s October, and you see something on TV that makes you want to throw your remote. You could even be one of the people who decides to lend a hand the night before Election Day, when the polls have inevitably tightened and the race is too close to call.

But, when you sign up today, you will do more to help elect Hillary than if you got started in November.

**So don’t hesitate. Don’t wait for the “right moment.” If you want Hillary to be our next president, the best way to accomplish that is by becoming a volunteer today, when it’ll mean the most:** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=53&ms=MjYyODkzMDQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYzNDQ5NTkyS0&mt=2&rj=ODYzNDQ5NTkyS0&rt=0)
Thank you,

Brian

Brian Zuzenak

clean_body: | Peter -- Technically, Election Day is 81 days away. But real talk: In North Carolina and Florida, nearly 60 percent of votes will be cast before November 8th, either by mail or by folks voting early in person. Getting supporters to vote before Election Day is important for our path to victory -- it’s both easy for voters, and it helps our campaign better plan how to allocate our resources. It’s also a real lift in terms of our organization capacity. You’ve got to know which supporters to talk to, when to engage, how to contact them, and what to ask them to do. It requires us to be hitting on all cylinders. It takes data, analysis, field organizers, and a volunteer infrastructure that is efficient and effective without any operational hiccups. ** You could stay on the sidelines until the debates start in September. You could wait until it’s October, and you see something on TV that makes you want to throw your remote. You could even be one of the people who decides to lend a hand the night before Election Day, when the polls have inevitably tightened and the race is too close to call. But, when you sign up today, you will do more to help elect Hillary than if you got started in November. So don’t hesitate. Don’t wait for the “right moment.” If you want Hillary to be our next president, the best way to accomplish that is by becoming a volunteer today, when it’ll mean the most: Thank you, Brian Brian Zuzenak Virginia State Director Hillary for America | Be the first to know when Hillary is coming to your town -- sign up to get text updates: | Message & data rates apply; text HELP for help & STOP to cancel. | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 2 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House). name: Maggie Hassan
subject: all of this and more
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello there --
Our campaign is about ensuring that Washington works for Granite Staters, not corporate special interests. As Governor, I work every day to get things done the New Hampshire way -- reaching across party lines to solve problems and get results for hard-working families and small businesses. And it is long past time for Washington to take the same approach.
* We passed a bipartisan plan to expand health coverage to nearly fifty thousand hard-working Granite Staters. In Washington, I will fight to make health care more affordable and accessible for all Americans by building on and improving the Affordable Care Act.
* We've worked to help innovative businesses start up and grow through our "Live Free and Start" initiative, and we've invested in job training programs to remove barriers to good-paying employment -- and now our unemployment rate is among the lowest in the country. In the Senate, I'll work to bring this success nationwide by fighting to cut red tape for new companies and increase the R&D tax credit to help innovative businesses thrive.
* We've invested in our future innovators, entrepreneurs, and community leaders by making higher education more affordable and accessible. We froze in-state tuition at our universities for the first time in 25 years, and lowered tuition at community colleges. In the Senate, I will continue fighting to make college more affordable and reduce the burden of student loan debt through efforts like allowing students to refinance their loans, working to expand Pell Grants, and fighting to achieve debt-free public college for all families.

We can accomplish all of this and more in Washington, but only if we all step up to make it happen. [**I need your help if we're going to win this race. Please, will you contribute $3 or more to this campaign today to help bring New Hampshire's common sense to the Senate?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd79b/2a380d31/11e9e29a/fa4396/443026770/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwODE5X01XSF9uaC1uZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160819_MWH_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
I'm so proud of what this team has accomplished so far. Thanks again for fighting with me.
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

\-- _USA Today_ We've known since the beginning how critical this race is to our country's future -- control of the Senate hangs in the balance in Florida's Senate seat. And now, with Marco Rubio's off-the-charts fundraising numbers since he entered the race, this has gotten even more competitive. _USA Today_ reported that Marco Rubio's right-wing backers are outspending us at a rate of nearly **THREE TO ONE.**

And it's about to get even worse: The Republican Super PAC Senate Leadership Fund just promised Marco Rubio they'll spend at **AT LEAST $15 million **to buy him Florida's Senate seat.

**The reality is this: we 're being massively outspent, but we have a chance right now to fight back. **We need your help to do it. If we don't get everyone on board, including you, we risk handing Marco Rubio another term!

**_Phil Scott’s Week in Action_**

Greetings!

It was an exciting week for Team Scott as we look ahead to the November 8 election and the opportunity to meet with hundreds of Vermonters over the next dozen weeks to ensure we are their voice in this election! Phil will be spending the majority of these next few months on the road to listen to your challenges, hopes and ideas and make sure he never loses sight of who he’s fighting for! There is a clear choice in this election. Phil is the only candidate with a plan to improve the economy and make Vermont more affordable. We need your help to make sure the economy stays front and center and Vermonters recognize that Phil is the only choice to lead our state forward. Stay tuned for all of our activities and opportunities to participate!

Here are a few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**STATE REVENUE DOWN AGAIN – ENOUGH IS ENOUGH!**
There is a clear choice in this election and a new report this week that the General Fund fell short of projections, yet again, for the first month of the new fiscal year shown a spotlight on that choice. Phil is the **_only candidate_ who has a plan to **[**install truth in state budgeting**](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=85deb2da8b&e=19cebfe173)**and real fiscal responsibility.**

Relying on predictions that overestimate potential revenue far too **often is not a responsible way to budget** , and Phil will end this practice. Read Phil’s plan for truth in budgeting [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=07e9dac9bd&e=19cebfe173) and read the Campaign’s response to this week’s revenue report [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=98f76340cf&e=19cebfe173).
***
**MORE TRAGEDIES HIGHLIGHT OUR URGENT NEED FOR ACTION ON OPIODS**
Like all Vermonters, Phil and Team Scott were shocked and saddened to hear of the rash of drug-related overdoses in Barre in recent days. Our thoughts go out to the families impacted, as well as emergency responders, law enforcement and all those fighting to end this epidemic.

Phil issued a statement in response to ensure Vermonters know how he will work to combat opiate addiction, which you can view [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=00e70f3a93&e=19cebfe173). **There is no easy solution. This crisis effects every one of us, and we must work together to solve it**. Read Phil’s comprehensive plan to address our opiate crisis [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b02805ad2d&e=19cebfe173).
*******
**GEARING UP FOR THE GENERAL**
Team Scott is already in high gear for the General Election, but we want to make sure you are too. Keep up on our latest news by following us on [Twitter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=914e25a958&e=19cebfe173), [Facebook](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=dc35996e86&e=19cebfe173) and [Instagram](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ee9acf4424&e=19cebfe173), and join our mailing list at [www.philscott.org](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c8b550e279&e=19cebfe173). There are so many ways to help, from joining us at events, to putting up a lawn signs, to making a donation. We saw how ugly elections can get during the Primary, and how big donors can skew reality with misleading ads and false attacks. Phil will be ready to fight for you by keeping this election focused on his ideas and the clear choice in November. We hope you will join us as we continue on the high road and elect Phil Scott Governor of Vermont. Find out how you can join us [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=92cf713a9b&e=19cebfe173) and how you can support Phil [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b0f34a8429&e=19cebfe173).
*******
**AUGUST 22 DEBATE – COME SHOW YOUR SUPPORT FOR PHIL!**
On August 22, Phil will participate in the VT-NEA Gubernatorial Debate. Please come show your support for Phil as he participates in the first debate of the General Election.

The debate is on Monday, August 22 at Randolph’s**Chandler Music Hall.
We have VIP tickets available for Phil’s supporters to watch the debate in person! _We need you to be there for Phil_ and show your support for his campaign to _make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s top priorities_!

Audience members are encouraged to arrive at ****7:00pm****and the debate will take place from****7:30pm to 9:00pm****.
**
To reserve tickets in advance for this event, please [_click here_ ](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8e1502f0bb&e=19cebfe173)or contact [_miranda@philscott.org_](mailto:miranda@philscott.org)
***
**THE FIELD DAYS OF SUMMER**
Parade season and county Field Days are still in full swing and Team Scott is making the rounds to these vibrant celebrations around the state. This week we attended the **Addison County Field Days** , which has many wonderful agricultural exhibits, and walked in the **Bennington** parade. Phil and others also participated in **Harpoon’s Point to Point** bicycle race, and the **United Motorcyclists of Vermont 2016 Toy Run**.

**Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT** Catch us at the [Windsor County Chicken BBQ](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5a34c00c33&e=19cebfe173) this weekend, and make sure you tune in to the [VT-NEA Gubernatorial Debate](http://philscott.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1ce04fa2a1&e=19cebfe173) Monday, August 22. View our Events page [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=099163d3da&e=19cebfe173) for more information.
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_A Voice for Vermonters_**
“Scott's widespread support among Vermont small businesses helps him keep fundraising local: More than 81 percent of Scott's itemized contributions came from Vermonters (Source: Burlington Free Press: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e15d656e2f&e=19cebfe173))

**_Across Party Lines: A Leader for All Vermonters_**
“Lawmakers aren’t the only ones willing to cross party lines and vote for the Republican candidate for governor this year. Some voters say they like Scott because he has experience running a successful construction business.” (Source: Vermont Watchdog: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d808139180&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_It’s the Economy! Burlington Free Press & VT Digger Highlight Importance of Economic Focus_**
An editorial from the _Burlington Free Press_ and an article in _VT Digger_ this week both highlighted the critical need for this election to focus on the economy – elaborating on why economic issues are weighing most heavily on the minds of Vermonters. The _Free Press_ astutely points out: “The perennial revenue shortfall in Montpelier that forces budget adjustments — sometimes several times within a year — point to **a mismatch between spending priorities and the state’s ability to raise the money.** And despite some Vermonters' aversion to big business, the tax on corporate profits plays a key part in paying the state's bills.” Vermonters need a leader who recognizes this problem and knows how to fix it – that leader is Phil Scott. The paper also asserts, “Those who seek to serve Vermont must understand that a flourishing state will have more resources to meet the many challenges Vermont faces.” **This is exactly why strengthening our economy is Phil’s top priority!** Read the full editorial [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=41f9645af0&e=19cebfe173).

_VT Digger_ provides even more motivation for Phil, speaking to several residents of Bethel who shared their economic struggles from job loss to trouble paying bills. **This is who Phil is fighting for**! Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8fd6854de8&e=19cebfe173) and read more about Phil’s economic plans [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=6b1788e490&e=19cebfe173). ******* Thanks to everyone that came out to events this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ad2ba43d10&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d407aa27d1&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | Phil Scott’s Week in Action Greetings! It was an exciting week for Team Scott as we look ahead to the November 8 election and the opportunity to meet with hundreds of Vermonters over the next dozen weeks to ensure we are their voice in this election! Phil will be spending the majority of these next few months on the road to listen to your challenges, hopes and ideas and make sure he never loses sight of who he’s fighting for! There is a clear choice in this election. Phil is the only candidate with a plan to improve the economy and make Vermont more affordable. We need your help to make sure the economy stays front and center and Vermonters recognize that Phil is the only choice to lead our state forward. Stay tuned for all of our activities and opportunities to participate! Here are a few of this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign. STATE REVENUE DOWN AGAIN – ENOUGH IS ENOUGH! There is a clear choice in this election and a new report this week that the General Fund fell short of projections, yet again, for the first month of the new fiscal year shown a spotlight on that choice. Phil is the only candidate who has a plan to real fiscal responsibility. Relying on predictions that overestimate potential revenue far too often is not a responsible way to budget , and Phil will end this practice. Read Phil’s plan for truth in budgeting and read the Campaign’s response to this week’s revenue report . * MORE TRAGEDIES HIGHLIGHT OUR URGENT NEED FOR ACTION ON OPIODS Like all Vermonters, Phil and Team Scott were shocked and saddened to hear of the rash of drug-related overdoses in Barre in recent days. Our thoughts go out to the families impacted, as well as emergency responders, law enforcement and all those fighting to end this epidemic. Phil issued a statement in response to ensure Vermonters know how he will work to combat opiate addiction, which you can view . There is no easy solution. This crisis effects every one of us, and we must work together to solve it. Read Phil’s comprehensive plan to address our opiate crisis . GEARING UP FOR THE GENERAL Team Scott is already in high gear for the General Election, but we want to make sure you are too. Keep up on our latest news by following us on , and , and join our mailing list at . There are so many ways to help, from joining us at events, to putting up a lawn signs, to making a donation. We saw how ugly elections can get during the Primary, and how big donors can skew reality with misleading ads and false attacks. Phil will be ready to fight for you by keeping this election focused on his ideas and the clear choice in November. We hope you will join us as we continue on the high road and elect Phil Scott Governor of Vermont. Find out how you can join us and how you can support Phil . AUGUST 22 DEBATE – COME SHOW YOUR SUPPORT FOR PHIL! On August 22, Phil will participate in the VT-NEA Gubernatorial Debate. Please come show your support for Phil as he participates in the first debate of the General Election. The debate is on Monday, August 22 at Randolph’sChandler Music Hall. We have VIP tickets available for Phil’s supporters to watch the debate in person! We need you to be there for Phil and show your support for his campaign to make the economy and affordability Montpelier’s top priorities! Audience members are encouraged to arrive at **7:00pmand the debate will take place from7:30pm to 9:00pm. To reserve tickets in advance for this event, please contact mailto:miranda@philscott.org * THE FIELD DAYS OF SUMMER Parade season and county Field Days are still in full swing and Team Scott is making the rounds to these vibrant celebrations around the state. This week we attended the Addison County Field Days , which has many wonderful agricultural exhibits, and walked in the Bennington parade. Phil and others also participated in Harpoon’s Point to Point bicycle race, and the United Motorcyclists of Vermont 2016 Toy Run. Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT Catch us at the this weekend, and make sure you tune in to the Monday, August 22. View our Events page for more information. ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS A Voice for Vermonters “Scott's widespread support among Vermont small businesses helps him keep fundraising local: More than 81 percent of Scott's itemized contributions came from Vermonters (Source: Burlington Free Press: ) Across Party Lines: A Leader for All Vermonters “Lawmakers aren’t the only ones willing to cross party lines and vote for the Republican candidate for governor this year. Some voters say they like Scott because he has experience running a successful construction business.” (Source: Vermont Watchdog: ) ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS It’s the Economy! Burlington Free Press & VT Digger Highlight Importance of Economic Focus An editorial from the Burlington Free Press and an article in VT Digger this week both highlighted the critical need for this election to focus on the economy – elaborating on why economic issues are weighing most heavily on the minds of Vermonters. The Free Press astutely points out: “The perennial revenue shortfall in Montpelier that forces budget adjustments — sometimes several times within a year — point to a mismatch between spending priorities and the state’s ability to raise the money. And despite some Vermonters' aversion to big business, the tax on corporate profits plays a key part in paying the state's bills.” Vermonters need a leader who recognizes this problem and knows how to fix it – that leader is Phil Scott. The paper also asserts, “Those who seek to serve Vermont must understand that a flourishing state will have more resources to meet the many challenges Vermont faces.” This is exactly why strengthening our economy is Phil’s top priority! Read the full editorial . VT Digger provides even more motivation for Phil, speaking to several residents of Bethel who shared their economic struggles from job loss to trouble paying bills. This is who Phil is fighting for! Read the full article and read more about Phil’s economic plans . ***** Thanks to everyone that came out to events this week. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities everyday – and share your thoughts – and . name: Barack Obama
subject: Let’s be clear:
body:
Will you give it your all? | Peter --

We both know Donald Trump is unfit for the presidency.

But that doesn't mean he can't win in November -- not when his campaign and the Republican Party are raising millions of dollars every day to help him beat Hillary Clinton, including more than $80 million last month alone.

So no matter what the polls and pundits say, Peter, we have to act like a team that's playing from behind -- because until the very last person in line casts his or her vote, nothing is certain.

That means doing everything we can to give our organizers on the ground all the support and resources they need to do the work it takes to win presidential campaigns. It means making phone calls or standing in front of grocery stores or knocking on doors. It means doing everything we can to make sure folks get registered. It means making sure people know why Hillary's the best candidate for this job, and making sure they make it to the ballot box on Election Day.

**Let's give it everything we've got, Peter -- chip in to help make sure Hillary Clinton becomes the next President of the United States (and you’ll get a free sticker in the deal, too):** [](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjYyOTI1OTYS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODYzNDk5Nzg5S0&mt=1&rt=0)
Thanks -- now let's get this done.

Barack

|
